kaResponse.analysis: Pod kv-store-2 is permanently unschedulable because its PVC data-kv-store-2 was manually created with a nonexistent StorageClass 'broken-storage-class' instead of the valid 'lvms-vg1', causing repeated ProvisioningFailed events (16x) and blocking the third StatefulSet replica from ever binding its volume.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Pod kv-store-2 is permanently unschedulable because its PVC data-kv-store-2 was manually created with a nonexistent StorageClass 'broken-storage-class' instead of the valid 'lvms-vg1', causing repeated ProvisioningFailed events (16x) and blocking the third StatefulSet replica from ever binding its volume.
causalChain: Signal: KubeStatefulSetReplicasMismatch — StatefulSet kv-store has 3 desired replicas but only 2 ready/available
Why? Pod kv-store-2 is in Pending state with FailedScheduling: '0/4 nodes are available: pod has unbound immediate PersistentVolumeClaims'
Why? PVC data-kv-store-2 is stuck in Pending phase — ProvisioningFailed 16 times: 'storageclass.storage.k8s.io "broken-storage-class" not found'
Why? PVC data-kv-store-2 was manually created (manager: kubectl-create) with storageClassName: 'broken-storage-class' — replacing the original LVMS-provisioned PVC

Root cause: A manual kubectl-create operation created PVC data-kv-store-2 with a nonexistent StorageClass 'broken-storage-class'. The cluster's only valid StorageClass is 'lvms-vg1'. The PVC must be deleted and recreated with storageClassName: lvms-vg1 to restore the third replica.

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Remediation target is PVC data-kv-store-2 in demo-statefulset — this is the exact resource whose storageClassName configuration is wrong and must be fixed (deleted and recreated with correct class). The StatefulSet itself does not need modification; its volumeClaimTemplate correctly uses the default dynamic provisioner.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Single PVC is the sole blocker. Fixing data-kv-store-2's StorageClass will immediately unblock scheduling of kv-store-2 and resolve the replica mismatch. No other resources need modification.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: StatefulSet spec and status, all 3 pods (status, events), all 3 PVCs (spec, status, events), all StorageClasses in the cluster, StatefulSet events. Checked node assignment for healthy pods. The investigation is complete — no upstream or downstream gaps identified.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced the full chain: alert → pod Pending → PVC unbound → StorageClass not found → manual PVC creation with wrong storageClassName. Confirmed the StorageClass inventory (only lvms-vg1 exists). Cannot go deeper — this is a human/automation configuration error at the PVC creation layer.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by direct tool evidence: (1) PVC spec shows storageClassName: broken-storage-class, manager: kubectl-create; (2) StorageClass list shows only lvms-vg1 exists; (3) PVC events show 16x ProvisioningFailed with 'storageclass not found'; (4) Pod events show FailedScheduling due to unbound PVC; (5) data-kv-store-0 and data-kv-store-1 PVC specs confirm lvms-vg1 is the correct class and both are Bound. No inaccessible data sources for this investigation.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for this target (both tier1 and tier2 history are empty per get_namespaced_resource_context).
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) Node resource exhaustion — ruled out: pods kv-store-0 and kv-store-1 are running on different nodes with no pressure events; (2) Image pull failure — ruled out: pod events show image already present and container was Created/Started before deletion; (3) ConfigMap misconfiguration — ruled out: kv-store-0 and kv-store-1 use the same ConfigMap successfully; (4) Resource quota exhaustion — no evidence; the scheduler error explicitly cites the unbound PVC as the only blocker.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.97: Starting at 1.0. -0.03 for not being able to confirm exactly which operator or human created the replacement PVC with the wrong class (the 'why' behind the manual kubectl-create is not directly observable, but does not affect the fix). All other claims are fully evidence-backed.

contributingFactors: PVC data-kv-store-2 was created manually via kubectl-create (not by the StatefulSet controller), with an incorrect storageClassName: 'broken-storage-class'
The StorageClass 'broken-storage-class' does not exist in the cluster — only 'lvms-vg1' (topolvm.io provisioner) is present
Provisioning failure event repeated 16 times: 'storageclass.storage.k8s.io "broken-storage-class" not found'
StatefulSet PVC retention policy is 'Retain' on scale, so the original data-kv-store-2 PVC (provisioned by LVMS) was replaced with the manually created broken PVC
StatefulSet uses OrderedReady pod management, so kv-store-2 blocking prevents any future scale-out beyond 2 replicas
